execution_queue.primary_operator_action: Run the production-like VS2 integrated security rehearsal in a controlled namespace.
execution_queue.required_proof_package: RequestContext trace, PostgreSQL/RLS evidence, OPA decision logs, egress transcript, backup/restore evidence, and audit refs.
execution_queue.stop_or_reject_when: Any cross-namespace read, unexpected egress, missing audit ref, unredacted secret, or unverifiable restore appears.

execution_queue.primary_operator_action: Exercise backup, restore, replay, cursor reconciliation, and audit durability after the security rehearsal.
execution_queue.required_proof_package: Backup logs, restore logs, reconciled cursor state, replay result, and immutable audit verification.
execution_queue.stop_or_reject_when: Replay changes external state unexpectedly, cursors diverge, audit continuity is broken, or restore evidence is incomplete.

execution_queue.primary_operator_action: Rehearse live GitHub read-only connector access against selected repositories.
execution_queue.required_proof_package: Redacted permission selection, selected repository list, read-only call ledger, delivery artifacts, audit refs, and zero-write proof.
execution_queue.stop_or_reject_when: Any write-capable scope is granted, a mutation endpoint is called, repository scope is wider than approved, or audit refs are missing.

execution_queue.primary_operator_action: Walk through physical macOS permission grant, first sample capture, pause, and revoke.
execution_queue.required_proof_package: Recording, screenshots, status transcript, first sample, pause timestamp, revoke timestamp, and operator notes.
execution_queue.stop_or_reject_when: Capture starts before consent, pause/revoke is ineffective, status is misleading, or sensitive data is exposed in proof artifacts.

execution_queue.primary_operator_action: Review real Chrome permission and privacy behavior.
execution_queue.required_proof_package: Recording, screenshots, browser permission pages, event timeline, issue log, and accept/reject note.
execution_queue.stop_or_reject_when: Browser state is captured without clear consent, denied permission is bypassed, degraded mode is unclear, or redaction is insufficient.

execution_queue.primary_operator_action: Execute one approved live non-GitHub Action with an explicit human approval record.
execution_queue.required_proof_package: Approval record, redacted request/result, provider receipt, state delta, idempotency record, audit refs, and GitHub exclusion proof.
execution_queue.stop_or_reject_when: Approval is ambiguous, provider receipt is missing, idempotency cannot be demonstrated, or the action uses GitHub instead of the selected non-GitHub provider.

execution_queue.primary_operator_action: Run a connected-source usability and trust study after the safety gates have evidence.
execution_queue.required_proof_package: Timed task notes, screenshots, recording, scoring rubric, trust/risk comprehension notes, and acceptance decision.
execution_queue.stop_or_reject_when: Operators cannot explain source scope, evidence basis, risk labels, or action consequences without assistance.

required_before_pass: Attach dated human records for H01 through H07 before changing any human row to PASS.
Keep live-provider, physical-device, browser privacy, production-like VS2, live Action, UX/trust, and recovery evidence separate.

execution_queue_item.primary_operator_action: Rehearse live GitHub read-only connector access against selected repositories.
execution_queue_item.required_proof_package: Redacted permission selection, selected repository list, read-only call ledger, delivery artifacts, audit refs, and zero-write proof.
execution_queue_item.stop_or_reject_when: Any write-capable scope is granted, a mutation endpoint is called, repository scope is wider than approved, or audit refs are missing.

format_rules.review_timestamp: ISO-8601 timestamp with timezone, for example 2026-06-24T12:00:00Z

evidence_packet_manifest.required_evidence: Redacted GitHub App or equivalent least-privilege installation permission snapshot.
Selected repository list and explicit confirmation that no unselected repositories were ingested.
Read-only call ledger showing zero write-capable HTTP methods or endpoints.
Connector Delivery, Artifact/Evidence, and audit refs produced from selected repositories.
Independent zero-write proof covering permissions, declared actions, routes, UI/CLI, and observed calls.

senior_review_perspective_sequence.decision: Verify that live GitHub evidence supports one CornerStone connected-source workflow without exposing ConnectorHub as a second product.
Keep Product code behind ConnectorPort; GitHub permissions and provider clients stay inside the ConnectorHub boundary.
Require selected-repository source refs, Projection/Delivery refs, Artifact/Evidence refs, and audit refs for every accepted live item.
Capture a call ledger, delivery receipts, freshness/failure state, and replayable audit evidence before accepting live-read readiness.
Reject the gate if any write permission, write endpoint, credential leak, unselected repository access, or raw provider payload exposure appears.
Treat the human record as evidence that live provider semantics match the local CS-CH-015 through CS-CH-020 fixture contracts.

senior_review_perspective_sequence.decision: Confirm that capture earns trust because users can see, pause, revoke, and understand observation state.
Keep platform permission state as ConnectorHub input evidence, not as hidden Product authority.
Require consent, permission, source state, sample, lifecycle decision, and audit refs for the walkthrough.
Record timestamps for grant, first sample, pause, revoke, and blocked post-revoke sample attempts.
Reject if capture starts without explicit consent or continues after pause or revoke.
Compare physical-device behavior against CS-CH-021, CS-CH-022, and CS-CH-027 local fixture contracts.

senior_review_perspective_sequence.decision: Confirm the browser capture experience is understandable without exposing internal ConnectorHub mechanics.
Keep extension input behind ConnectorPort and backend policy validation.
Require active-tab payload, allowlist config, sensitive-page decision, lifecycle decision, and audit refs.
Record a timeline that distinguishes manual capture, auto capture, block/degrade, pause, and revoke.
Reject if sensitive pages leak, capture happens outside allowlist, or users cannot understand permissions.
Compare the review against CS-CH-024, CS-CH-025, CS-CH-026, and CS-CH-027 fixture contracts.

senior_review_perspective_sequence.decision: Confirm connected-source release claims do not outrun tenant, policy, egress, and recovery evidence.
Verify Product, Archive, and Connector boundaries hold under real RequestContext and deployment topology.
Require scenario-specific DB, policy, network, backup, restore, and audit transcripts.
Treat generated VS2 reports as inputs and require direct report, transcript, and audit integrity review.
Reject if RLS, OPA, egress deny, secret custody, or audit integrity is simulated where production-like proof is required.
Keep this gate separate from local VS2 proof and from ConnectorHub fixture PASS claims.

senior_review_perspective_sequence.decision: Confirm a governed live Action creates useful outcome evidence without normalizing autonomous external mutation.
Keep execution behind declared actions, policy, approval, idempotency, and ConnectorHub-mediated provider access.
Require ActionCard, policy decision, approval, result receipt, outcome evidence, idempotency, and audit refs.
Require provider receipt/state evidence and a rollback or compensation plan for the reversible target.
Reject if GitHub is used, approval is missing, the provider target is not reversible, or credentials leak.
Compare live execution semantics against CS-CH-029 through CS-CH-033 fixture contracts.

senior_review_perspective_sequence.decision: Confirm users can complete Connected Sources and Capture Inbox tasks as one CornerStone product.
Ensure setup, evidence, policy, and audit details are progressively disclosed instead of connector-admin-first.
Require task script, participant profile, timed notes, issue list, and acceptance decision refs.
Capture enough trace material to reproduce confusion, trust gaps, and recovery paths.
Reject if users misunderstand capture scope, approval boundaries, raw access, or external action risk.
Use the study to decide whether ConnectorHub concepts are adoptable in CornerStone UI language.

lifecycle_steps.action: Review each senior-review perspective and collect missing external evidence before judging the gate.
lifecycle_steps.evidence_required: Filled senior_review_perspective_findings for every required role.

lifecycle_steps.action: Exercise backup, restore, replay, cursor reconciliation, and audit durability after the security rehearsal.
lifecycle_steps.evidence_required: Backup logs, restore logs, reconciled cursor state, replay result, and immutable audit verification.

lifecycle_steps.action: Run the minimum external rehearsal needed to satisfy this scenario's required human record.
lifecycle_steps.evidence_required: Every required field and evidence-packet manifest row is filled with redacted refs.

lifecycle_steps.action: Record issues, exceptions, or remediation needs instead of accepting a partial rehearsal.
lifecycle_steps.evidence_required: issues_or_exceptions plus reject/blocker notes when any stop condition appears.

lifecycle_steps.action: cornerstone connector human-gate validate-record --scenario CS-CH-H07 --record-file <filled-json> --json
lifecycle_steps.evidence_required: Structural validation envelope remains HUMAN_REQUIRED and redacted.

lifecycle_steps.action: Store the completed reviewer record, validation envelope, and updated scenario result trail.
lifecycle_steps.evidence_required: Dated ACCEPT or REJECT reviewer record plus validation ref.

lifecycle_steps.action: Use the next selector to advance only after dependency unlock evidence exists.
lifecycle_steps.evidence_required: Structurally valid ACCEPT validation refs for all dependency gates.

required_evidence: Backup manifest and restore logs for production-like durable state.
Cursor reconciliation and replay results.
Before/after logical counts and hashes.
Audit verification proving recovered state integrity.

senior_review_perspective_sequence.decision: Confirm CornerStone can recover connected-source knowledge without hidden loss or duplicate logical truth.
Verify Archive, Connector, search, quarantine, cursor, and audit recovery boundaries remain coherent.
Require backup manifest, restore log, cursor reconciliation, replay results, count/hash comparisons, and audit refs.
Use before/after counts and hashes plus audit verification to prove recovery, not operator narrative alone.
Reject if restore leaks cross-namespace data, resurrects deleted restricted content, or exposes secrets.
Convert exercise gaps into durable runbook/tooling requirements before production operations readiness.

scenario_delivery_unit_plan.stop_or_reject_when: Replay changes external state unexpectedly, cursors diverge, audit continuity is broken, or restore evidence is incomplete.

scenario_results.stop_or_reject_when: Replay changes external state unexpectedly, cursors diverge, audit continuity is broken, or restore evidence is incomplete.
scenario_results.title: Human recovery exercise
